evidence.label: Primary CVD/HFH
evidence.text: The primary outcome occurred in 914 patients (21.8%) in the sacubitril-valsartan group and 1117 (26.5%) in the enalapril group (HR 0.80; 95% CI, 0.73 to 0.87; P<0.001).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Patients must give written informed consent before any assessment is performed.~* Outpatients ≥ 18 years of age, male or female.~* Patients with a diagnosis of CHF NYHA class II-IV and reduced ejection fraction (EF =< 35%) and elevated BNP.~* Patients must be on an ACEI or an ARB at a stable dose of at least enalapril 10 mg/d or equivalent for at least 4 weeks.~* Patients must be treated with a β-blocker, unless contraindicated or not tolerated, at a stable dose for at le...

allOutcomes.title: CV death or total HF hospitalizations

evidence.label: Primary CVD/HFH
evidence.text: Total primary events were 894 in the sacubitril-valsartan group and 1009 in the valsartan group (rate ratio 0.87; 95% CI, 0.75 to 1.01; P=0.06).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) ≥45% by echo during screening epoch or within 6 months prior to study entry.~* Symptom(s) of heart failure (HF) and requiring treatment with diuretic(s) for HF at least 30 days prior to study entry.~* Current symptom(s) of HF (NYHA class II-IV)~* Structural heart disease (left atrial enlargement or left ventricular hypertrophy) documented by echocardiogram.~* Elevated NT-proBNP~Exclusion Criteria:~* Any prior measurement of LVEF <...

evidence.label: Primary CVD/HF
evidence.text: The primary outcome occurred in 338 patients (11.9%) in the sacubitril-valsartan group and 373 (13.2%) in the ramipril group (HR 0.90; 95% CI, 0.78 to 1.04; P=0.17).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Male or female patients ≥ 18 years of age.~2. Diagnosis of spontaneous AMI based on the universal MI definition* with randomization to occur between 12 hours and 7 days after index event presentation. (*patients with spontaneous MI event determined to be secondary to another medical condition such as anemia, hypotension, or arrhythmia OR thought to be caused by coronary vasospasm with document normal coronary arteries are not eligible; patients with clinical presentatio...

evidence.label: NT-proBNP
evidence.text: Sacubitril-valsartan resulted in a greater time-averaged reduction in NT-proBNP than valsartan (ratio of change 0.85; 95% CI, 0.74 to 0.98).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 99 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "INCLUSION CRITERIA:~1. Signed informed consent must be obtained prior to participation in the study~2. Patients >=18 years of age, male or female~3. Current hospitalization for Worsening Heart Failure (WHF) (HFpEF decompensation), or within 30 days of discharge following a WHF event (defined as hospitalization, emergency department (ED) visit or out-of-hospital urgent HF visit, all requiring IV diuretics). title: RapidMeta Cardiology | Sacubitril/Valsartan (ARNI) in HF Ultra-Precision v12.5
